concept.conceptName: Ammonia|Mass Concentration|Moment in time|Blood, Serum or Plasma
Ammonia|Substance Concentration|Moment in time|Blood, Serum or Plasma

en.name: Ammonia in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Ammonia (NH3) is produced in the body as a by-product of amino-acid metabolism and processed in the liver into urea, which is eliminated in the urine [1]. Elevated blood ammonia is toxic to the central nervous system and is most often measured in suspected hepatic encephalopathy (severe liver disease), Reye syndrome, urea-cycle disorders, and other inborn errors of metabolism [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on plasma or whole blood — and increasingly by point-of-care blood-gas devices on arterial blood. Results are reported either in mass units (**µg/dL**) or in SI units (**µmol/L**, the unit recognised by NPU as the standard for substance concentration of ammonia/ammonium [2]). ## Included Concepts The set contains five LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, all measuring ammonia in blood, grouped here by specimen. ### Plasma (default specimen for routine biochemistry) The most common specimen for routine ammonia measurement. EDTA or heparin plasma is preferred over serum because clotting introduces additional ammonia. - *3036887 / 22763-7 — Ammonia [Mass/volume] in Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in µg/dL. - *3011958 / 16362-6 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. ### Blood (whole blood, unspecified) Used when the source reports ammonia on whole blood without specifying arterial origin — typically a co-oximetry or dedicated blood-gas channel on a point-of-care device. - *3030942 / 32664-5 — Ammonia [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: reported in µg/dL. - *3005347 / 1839-0 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. ### Arterial blood Used when the specimen is explicitly an arterial blood sample. Only the SI variant exists in LOINC. - *1002144 / 93684-9 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows the LOINC property: mass-concentration concepts use **µg/dL** (concept 8837), substance-concentration concepts use **µmol/L** (concept 8749). Conversion: 1 µg/dL ≈ 0.587 µmol/L; 1 µmol/L ≈ 1.703 µg/dL (molar mass of NH3 = 17.031 g/mol). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the two LOINC groups for ammonia in blood/serum/plasma (mass concentration and substance concentration) and pulling in all their descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Ammonium ion (NH4+)** concepts (`1844-0`, `1845-7`). LOINC distinguishes these from `Ammonia` even though clinical labs measure them as the same physical quantity. Map ammonia results to the `Ammonia` concepts above; if a source labels the test specifically as "ammonium ion", consider whether a separate concept set is needed. - **Other specimens** such as urine, dialysis fluid, amniotic fluid — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Unrelated panels** like `Gas and ammonia panel - Arterial blood` (`100847-3`) or histidine ammonia lyase activity — out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "ammonia", "NH3" or similar without further specification: `3036887 / 22763-7 — Ammonia [Mass/volume] in Plasma` if the value is in µg/dL, `3011958 / 16362-6 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Plasma` if the value is in µmol/L. Plasma is the default specimen for routine biochemistry analysers. - **Whole blood (point-of-care)**: if the source explicitly reports "blood ammonia" from a point-of-care device, map to `3030942 / 32664-5` (mg-equivalent in µg/dL) or `3005347 / 1839-0` (µmol/L). - **Arterial blood** (e.g. blood-gas analyser): map to `1002144 / 93684-9 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood`. Since no mass-concentration variant exists in LOINC for arterial blood, convert µg/dL to µmol/L (×0.587) before mapping. - **Do not confuse with**: ammonium ion concepts (`1844-0`, `1845-7`) — these are sematically distinct in LOINC even though clinically equivalent — or ammonia in non-blood specimens. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: the source lab's measurement method (enzymatic glutamate dehydrogenase, ion-selective electrode, dry chemistry, etc.) can be preserved in the measurement source value but does not change which concept to map to. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 1839-0 — Ammonia [Moles/volume] in Blood (Part description for "Ammonia"). Available: https://loinc.org/1839-0/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
